km.Yorkshire, England: យ៉ុរកស៊ីរe, ប្រទេសអង់គ្លេស, uណៃteត គីnហ្គដុម

zh.lifeSummary: Matilda FitzWalter於大約1174年與Gerard de Furnival在英國越可爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Matilda FitzWalter于大约1174年与Gerard de Furnival在英国越可爾结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。

nameMeanings.name: FitzWalter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman, English, Irish: relationship name from Anglo-Norman French fiz ‘son’ ( see Fitz ) + the personal name Walter , ‘son of Walter’.

variants.name: Fitzwater
meanings: Irish: altered form of Fitzwalter, a patronymic from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Walter , meaning ‘son of Walter’.

History: This is the name of a family of Norman origin, descended from Godfrey, Count of Brionne (died c. 1010). Godfrey's grandson, Richard FitzGilbert, accompanied William the Conqueror and was made Lord of Clare. His great-grandson was Robert FitzWalter, one of the barons who guaranteed the Magna Carta in 1215.

variants.name: Walter
meanings: English (of Norman origin), German, Dutch, Polish, Swedish, and Danish: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements wald ‘rule’ + heri, hari ‘army’. This personal name was introduced into England both in the reign of Edward the Confessor and by the Normans (in the forms Walt(i)er, Waut(i)er). After the Norman Conquest it soon became one of the most popular personal names in Britain. Compare Walther .
Germanized or Americanized form of Czech, Slovak, Croatian, and Slovenian Valter and Czech Valtr: from the personal name Valter, Valtr, of ancient Germanic origin (see 1 above).

variants.name: Fitz
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

nameMeanings.name: Matilda
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

variants.name: Mathilda
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

en.lifeSummary: Matilda FitzWalter married Gerard de Furnival about 1174, in Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son.

fr.lifeSummary: Matilda FitzWalter a épousé Gerard de Furnival environ 1174, à Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils.

de.lifeSummary: Matilda FitzWalter hat um 1174 in Yorkshire, England Gerard de Furnival geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Matilda FitzWalter lei sposato Gerard de Furnival circa 1174, nel Yorkshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio.

ja.Yorkshire, England: イギリスイギリスヨークシュアイア
ja.lifeSummary: Matilda FitzWalterは約1174年にイギリスヨークシュアイアでGerard de Furnivalと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Matilda FitzWalter(은)는 대략 1174년 영국 요르크실르에서 Gerard de Furnival(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Matilda FitzWalter casou-se com Gerard de Furnival aproximadamente 1174, em Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho.

ru.lifeSummary: Matilda FitzWalter вышла замуж за человека по имени Gerard de Furnival около 1174 г. в местности еркшир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын.

es.lifeSummary: Matilda FitzWalter se casó con Gerard de Furnival aproximadamente 1174, en Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo.
